channel.title: Nicotine Science and Policy
channel.description: Daily digest of views, debates and discussions on nicotine science, policy, regulation and advocacy.

item.title: OPINION: The Kubin report, how Brussels works, and the right thing to do.
item.description: The Kubin report has become another test of whether Brussels is willing to listen to consumers, evidence and real-world outcomes. Critics argue that EU nicotine policy is too often shaped by institutional process, political pressure and precautionary instincts rather than a clear assessment of relative risk. For smokers, the right thing to do is not to restrict safer alternatives until cigarettes remain the easiest option. Better regulation should protect young people while preserving access to products that can move adults away from combustion. (Published on June 16, 2026byclearingtheair.eu)

item.title: PDNW Warns EU: Vape and Nicotine Prohibition Will Hand Market to Criminals
item.description: Prohibition does not remove demand for vapes or nicotine products — it hands the market to criminals. When legal access is restricted, consumers often turn to unregulated channels where product safety, age controls and tax revenue disappear. The result is weaker oversight, stronger illicit trade and fewer realistic routes away from combustible cigarettes. Effective nicotine policy should regulate safer alternatives proportionately, not push them underground. (Published on June 17, 2026byprohibitiondoesnotwork.com)

item.title: Netherlands throws out disposable vapes ban plan
item.description: The Netherlands’ decision to drop its disposable vape ban plan shows the limits of prohibition-style nicotine policy. Concerns over enforcement, illicit trade and consumer behaviour appear to have outweighed the appeal of a simple ban. If adult smokers are to move away from cigarettes, regulation must keep lower-risk alternatives within legal, accountable channels. Better policy should target youth access and illegal products without removing safer options from the regulated market. (Published on June 17, 2026byclearingtheair.eu)
